commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR changes Codex app-server completion-stall handling to retry replay-safe idle failures, surface unsafe stalls as recoverable timeout results, retire timed-out native state, count native response deltas as activity, and update focused docs/tests.

PR surface: Source +64, Tests +166, Docs +10. Total +240 across 14 files.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review blockers: source inspection shows unscoped response deltas bypass turn correlation and timeout cleanup clears the binding unconditionally. The original user bug also has redacted live proof in the PR body and linked issue context.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] The remaining blockers are concrete file-level repairs that an automated fix lane can attempt before maintainer merge review.

target.title: fix(codex): prevent false completion stalls during native streams
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep raw assistant progress on the documented `turn/completed` terminal guard; progress text is still not treated as a final answer.
- Count forwarded native Codex response stream deltas, including `response.custom_tool_call_input.delta`, as active turn activity and attempt progress while a turn is already open.
- Keep the post-tool raw-assistant fallback timeout conservative at `5 * 60_000` for app-server/native paths that do not forward stream deltas.
- Preserve the recovery behavior for actual completion stalls: replay only replay-safe attempts, and for side-effecting/non-replay-safe stalls retire the stuck app-server client, clear stale native thread binding, release the lane, and surface a recoverable incomplete-turn result.
- Update focused coverage and in-code comments for the replay-safe, side-effecting, and native-delta timeout paths.

## Why this shape
The failure is not that raw assistant progress needs to become deliverable final text. The failure is that native Codex can spend a long time actively streaming a large tool input/patch after a raw assistant progress item has completed, before any completed tool-call notification exists. Without counting those `response.*.delta` events as activity, OpenClaw can falsely fire `turn.completion_idle_timeout` while the model is still working.

This keeps the lifecycle semantics strict: final delivery still requires `turn/completed`; active native stream deltas only keep the watchdog from killing a live turn.

## Before-fix proof (redacted)
Observed in affected Codex app-server trajectory/native logs. Timestamps are UTC unless noted.

- A side-effecting Telegram/Codex run completed tool calls and then emitted raw assistant progress text such as `The data model needs to change...` or `I'm changing the data model now...`.
- OpenClaw then timed out waiting for `turn/completed` with `lastActivityReason=notification:rawResponseItem/completed`, proving the raw assistant text was mid-turn progress, not a safe final answer.
- A deeper native Codex log check of the later recurrence showed the turn was not idle: Codex was actively streaming hundreds of `response.custom_tool_call_input.delta` websocket events for a large custom tool input when OpenClaw injected `<turn_aborted>`.
- Root cause for that recurrence: the OpenClaw completion-idle watchdog was blind to native stream deltas, so a live large patch/tool-input stream could look idle from the gateway's completed-notification view.

## Live validation
Latest live validation from Keshav on 2026-05-29 IST:

- Built and restarted gateway from this PR branch at head `7e0e908fb1`.
- Used a local-only 30s post-tool raw-assistant fallback timeout to make the delta-stream path easy to exercise; that timeout was not committed or pushed.
- Reattempted the Vietnam webpage edit flow that had repeatedly failed with `turn.completion_idle_timeout` after raw assistant progress.
- The reattempt completed successfully without the prior idle-timeout error.
- The branch remains at the production fallback limit, `5 * 60_000`.

## Tests / validation
- Earlier validation on the rebased PR branch: `git diff --check`, `oxfmt --check` on changed TypeScript files, and focused Codex app-server/recovery vitest coverage passed (`5` files / `67` tests).
- After the latest native-delta activity commit `7e0e908fb1`: `git diff --check` passed and the commit hook formatter ran. Per operator instruction, no test suite/build/restart was run by the bot after that patch.
- Live gateway validation was then performed manually by Keshav as described above.

## Related issue
- Related to #87744. This PR addresses the Codex-backed Telegram completion-idle family; the latest live proof narrows one concrete false-timeout cause to native response delta streams not being counted as turn activity.
target.author: keshavbotagent

commands.reason: repair commands require a ClawSweeper PR or a PR opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge

commands.author: keshavbotagent
commands.reason: comment version already processed in ledger
